evidence_excerpt: Persistent congenital maxillary/mandibular dysplasia with ongoing dental management plans (retain primary teeth, consider implants after puberty); no reported deterioration or major complications; facial palsy noted to be improving over time.

quality_of_life.evidence_excerpt: Homolateral hypopigmentation and facial palsy have persisted, with the palsy noted as improving over time; no severe symptom reports.

timeline_events.evidence_excerpt: Completed phase one of orthodontic treatment consisting of alignment of the anterior teeth.
The palsy affecting the left cheek has been improving over time.

notes_on_uncertainty: Exact diagnosis date and follow-up intervals are not provided. Functional impact and symptom severity are not explicitly detailed; classifications are inferred conservatively from the case narrative. Timing of orthodontic phase completion relative to index time is unclear, so classified as unknown.
